title: gcs driver backend config
description: Schema for the config: block under a backend entry whose driver is gcs. Control plane authenticates via Application Default Credentials (GOOGLE_APPLICATION_CREDENTIALS on the controller Deployment; STORAGE_EMULATOR_HOST is honoured for emulators); the credential fields here are the S3-interop data-plane pair written to access Secrets.

project.description: GCP project ID buckets are created in. Also written to access Secrets under the project key.

endpoint.description: S3-interop endpoint written to access Secrets. Defaults to https://storage.googleapis.com. Data planes with residency requirements should set the regional host matching the buckets' location, https://storage.<region>.rep.googleapis.com, from which SigV4 signing regions derive. Also override for emulators (fake-gcs-server) so consumers reach the same endpoint the controller provisions against; the control plane itself follows ADC/STORAGE_EMULATOR_HOST, not this field.

region.description: Optional SigV4 signing region written to access Secrets under the region key, so consumers source it from the one Secret instead of deriving it from the endpoint hostname. Should match the regional endpoint and the buckets' location parameter (e.g. europe-west4).

accessKeyID.description: Static HMAC access key ID copied into every access Secret. Mint out of band for a service account with access to this backend's buckets: gcloud storage hmac create <sa-email>. The service account's grant defines what every access can do; data planes that clean up after themselves (e.g. staged-write archival) need roles/storage.objectAdmin (get/create/delete), not just read+create. envsubst:"true" - supports ${VAR} interpolation at controller startup.

secretAccessKey.description: Static HMAC secret copied into every access Secret. envsubst:"true" - supports ${VAR} interpolation at controller startup.
